description: Helicopter scenic flights and charters. Operations are complex — each machine has fixed capacity, pilots need manifests before departure, and multiple machines may need filling simultaneously.
key_characteristics: Fixed machine capacity per flight (typically 4–6 passengers)
Pilot manifest required per flight before departure
Multiple machines may run simultaneously — allocation across machines is daily logistics
Passenger details needed ahead of time for pre-flight admin
pain_points: Pilot manifest and machine allocation on the day is manual or managed outside the booking system
Filling each machine optimally requires juggling bookings manually across departure times
Weather cancellations create a cascade of rescheduling that's hard to manage without a proper system
Passenger details (name, weight if required) aren't captured at booking in basic systems
Multi-machine operations can't be viewed in a single booking interface
general_discovery_questions: How do you manage your pilot manifest — is that generated from your booking system or done separately?
When you have multiple machines running on the same day, how do you allocate passengers across them?
What happens to your bookings when you get a weather cancellation — how do you handle the rescheduling?
How far in advance do you need passenger details for your pre-flight admin?
fareharbor_strengths: Customisable manifest fields including passenger details per booking
Multi-resource allocation — manage multiple machines from one view
